creator: jkoshy@FreeBSD.org
text:
> 	"Working" version 110.9.1 didn't.  (Died on divide by zero
> 	exceptions, etc.)  This is the latest stable release from
> 	Nov. 1999.  Note: some CM interfaces differ between the two.
> 	Also note: patch includes NEW FILE scripts/mkplist.

Can you tell me which version of FreeBSD you are running?  $(uname -a),
time of the last buildworld, and all that stuff.

The SML/NJ port should work on both 3-STABLE and 4-STABLE.  There was
screwup in the core OS for a while on the 4-CURRENT branch (before it
became 4-STABLE) that had introduced an inadvertent change of behaviour in
FPU error reporting. [Basically, integer overflow was being reported as a
divide-by-zero].

I have otherwise found SML/NJ v110.9.1 to be quite stable.  If you are
suffering from some other bug, could you please send over a small test
case?  I have been thinking of forking the port into SML/NJ-devel and
sml-nj-stable.  Maybe your bug will provide the justification :).

Regards,
Koshy
<jkoshy@freebsd.org>
